description:
You are a data-engineer who has just received three raw CSV files under
    /home/user/source_data.  
    Your goal is to archive the files, move the originals out of the way,
    re-hydrate the data to a staging area, and finally produce a manifest that
    upstream orchestration tools can validate.

    STEP-BY-STEP REQUIREMENTS
    1. Verify that the following three files exist *exactly* under
       /home/user/source_data (they should already be present before you start):
           customers.csv
           orders.csv
           products.csv

    2. Create the directory hierarchy
          /home/user/etl/archive
          /home/user/etl/raw_backup
          /home/user/etl/staging
          /home/user/etl/logs
       if any of them are missing.

    3. Pack the three CSV files into a gzip-compressed tarball named
          /home/user/etl/archive/source_data_20230630.tar.gz
       The tarball must **not** embed absolute paths; inside the archive each
       member should be the bare file name (e.g. “customers.csv” rather than
       “/home/user/source_data/customers.csv”).

    4. Move (do **not** copy) the original CSV files from
       /home/user/source_data into /home/user/etl/raw_backup, keeping the same
       file names.  After this step, /home/user/source_data must contain no CSV
       files, while /home/user/etl/raw_backup must contain all three.

    5. Extract the newly created tarball into
          /home/user/etl/staging/source_data_20230630/
       so that, after extraction, the path to customers.csv is
          /home/user/etl/staging/source_data_20230630/customers.csv
       (the same pattern holds for the other two files).

    6. Produce a manifest file called
          /home/user/etl/logs/source_data_20230630.manifest
       with the following exact text format, one entry per line, terminated by
       a single ‘\n’ newline (no extra blank line at the end):
          <filename>|<size_bytes>
       The list **must** be sorted alphabetically by the file name portion.
       Using the provided sample files, the correct manifest should look
       literally like this (including the final newline):

          customers.csv|22
          orders.csv|56
          products.csv|54

       – 22, 56 and 54 are the uncompressed byte-sizes of the respective files.
       – Do **not** include any other fields such as checksums or dates.

    7. Leave the tarball and all directories in place; they will be inspected
       by the automated grader.

    WHAT WILL BE AUTOMATICALLY CHECKED
    • The archive /home/user/etl/archive/source_data_20230630.tar.gz exists and
      is a valid gzip-compressed tar file containing exactly the three CSVs
      listed above, with no sub-directories inside the archive.  
    • /home/user/source_data is empty of CSV files.  
    • The three original CSVs are now located in
      /home/user/etl/raw_backup.  
    • The extraction directory
      /home/user/etl/staging/source_data_20230630/ contains the three CSVs with
      byte-for-byte identical contents to the originals.  
    • The manifest file exists at the specified path and contains *exactly* the
      three lines shown above, in the same order and with a trailing newline.

    HINTS (not mandatory)
    • `tar -czf` and `tar -xzf` can help with archiving and extraction.  
    • To get the uncompressed byte-size use `stat --printf="%s\n" <file>`.  
    • Make sure to redirect manifest output with `>` not `>>` so that the file
      contains only the required lines.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data for the automatic grader.
        Anything here is invisible to the agent.
    -->
    <!-- Pre-existing files before the agent starts -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/source_data/customers.csv" size="22">
id,name
1,Alice
2,Bob
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/source_data/orders.csv" size="56">
order_id,customer_id,total
1001,1,250.00
1002,2,175.50
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/source_data/products.csv" size="54">
product_id,name,price
10,Widget,25.00
20,Gadget,40.00
</file>
    </initial_files>

    <!-- Expected final artefacts -->
    <expected>
        <archive path="/home/user/etl/archive/source_data_20230630.tar.gz">
            <member name="customers.csv" size="22"/>
            <member name="orders.csv"    size="56"/>
            <member name="products.csv"  size="54"/>
        </archive>

        <dir path="/home/user/etl/raw_backup">
            <file name="customers.csv" size="22"/>
            <file name="orders.csv"    size="56"/>
            <file name="products.csv"  size="54"/>
        </dir>

        <dir path="/home/user/etl/staging/source_data_20230630">
            <file name="customers.csv" size="22"/>
            <file name="orders.csv"    size="56"/>
            <file name="products.csv"  size="54"/>
        </dir>

        <manifest path="/home/user/etl/logs/source_data_20230630.manifest">
customers.csv|22
orders.csv|56
products.csv|54
</manifest>
    </expected>
name: task_000000_917beeac
